name: override_detection_and_confirmation
description: Warm day, HVAC in cool mode at comfort_cool (74F). User bumps thermostat to heat mode at 14:00. After 10-minute confirm period passes (14:10), override is confirmed. Classification cycle at 14:30 is suppressed by active override.
notes: Tests the full override detection â†’ confirmation â†’ classification suppression lifecycle.
Gap 3 (override confirmation delay) is NOT yet in simulate.py â€” override_detected and override_confirmed assertions are marked track:integration.
What the simulator CAN test today: classification with manual_override_active=True produces no_action (not classification_applied).
The manual_override_active flag must be set via config initial state for the logic-track assertion to work.
Once Gap 3 is closed in simulate.py, the integration assertions can be promoted to logic-track.

events.note: Warm summer afternoon. Indoor at comfort_cool=74F. HVAC holding cool mode.
Warm-day classification: HVAC cool at comfort_cool=74F.
User bumps thermostat to heat mode. Override detection fires. 10-minute confirm window starts.
10 minutes have elapsed. Thermostat still in heat mode â†’ override confirmed.
Scheduled 30-min classification cycle. Override is active â€” should be suppressed.

assertions.reason: Initial warm-day classification applied: hvac_mode=cool, target=comfort_cool=74F.
Thermostat state changed to heat mode while classification says cool â€” override detection fires.
assertions.note: Requires Gap 3 (override confirmation delay) in simulate.py. Simulator thermostat_state_changed handler only manages stale-fan-clear, not override detection.

assertions.reason: 10 minutes elapsed with thermostat still in heat mode â†’ override_confirm_seconds=600 expired â†’ override confirmed.
assertions.note: Requires Gap 3 in simulate.py. Once gap is closed this becomes a logic-track assertion.

assertions.reason: Classification cycle while manual_override_active=True produces no_action (override suppresses classification). Simulator does implement this path in _handle_classification().

verdict.summary: Override detected on thermostat mode change, confirmed after 10-min delay, suppresses subsequent classification
verdict.observed_behavior: classification_applied (cool, 74F) â†’ override_detected (14:00) â†’ override_confirmed (14:10) â†’ no_action on classification (14:30)
verdict.expected_behavior: CA respects user's manual override and stops re-applying classification until override clears
